description: Fixed simulated focus-group panel for the /client-system letter. Modeled on Bertrand's ~10-year-old JVZoo buyer list (Youzign launch buyers, conditioned by years of affiliate JVZoo offers). Personas are reused verbatim every round so conversion deltas are real.

panel.name: Dale
panel.profile: Retired postal worker, Ohio. Bought Youzign in 2016 plus ~30 JVZoo tools since ($2k+ total). Opens every email, buys $17-$47 offers monthly, uses almost none. Wife teases him about his 'internet business'. Fears being the fool again; desires one thing that finally pays back.

panel.name: Priya
panel.profile: Virtual assistant in Bangalore earning $900/mo. Sharp, action-taker, already freelances on Upwork (admin tasks, $8/hr). $37 is a real expense. Skeptical of US-guru income claims but knows Upwork is real because she lives in it. Wants to move upmarket.

panel.name: Marcus
panel.profile: Laid-off IT helpdesk, Manchester UK. Severance running out, 2 kids. Bought 6 'AI side hustle' courses in 18 months, finished one. Cynical about 'AI gold rush' but quietly desperate for cash flow. Reads every word of long sales pages looking for the catch.

panel.name: Janet
panel.profile: Semi-retired bookkeeper, Florida. Youzign buyer day one. Tech-averse: can use Canva slowly, finds 'AI' intimidating. Refunds about a third of what she buys. Buys on warmth and trust of the seller, not logic. Has never used Upwork.

panel.name: Tunde
panel.profile: Lagos hustler, runs 3 side gigs (mini importation, P2P crypto, freelance graphics). Fast to buy under $50 if ROI story is concrete. Upwork payment/withdrawal frictions in Nigeria are a top concern nobody ever addresses. Allergic to fluff; skims to the price.

panel.name: Carol
panel.profile: Etsy seller (candles), Texas. On the list from a 2017 design-tool bridge page. Doesn't want clients — wants better product photos for HER shop. Buys anyway if she can repurpose the method. Budget-conscious, $37 is two candle sales.

panel.name: Steve
panel.profile: Affiliate marketer, Arizona. Promotes JVZoo offers himself; has seen ten thousand sales pages and written a hundred. Immune to scarcity timers and value stacks — judges congruence and proof quality. Buys competitor products to swipe them. Would buy purely to inspect the funnel if it smells competent.

panel.name: Mei
panel.profile: Shopify dropshipper, Vancouver. Was burned by Andromeda ad-cost spikes; KNOWS brands need creative volume because she is one. Could be the buyer OR realize she's the client and just hire someone. $37 trivial; her objection is time, not money.

panel.name: Raj
panel.profile: Accountant, New Jersey. 9 years on the list, opened maybe 200 emails, bought twice (Youzign, one $27 PLR pack). Risk-averse, needs guarantees spelled out, reads refund terms twice. Moves only when something feels like a sure, contained, small bet.

panel.name: Lisa
panel.profile: Struggling freelance designer, Portland. Proud of her craft, insulted by 'anyone can do this with AI' messaging — yet her Upwork proposals keep losing. Torn between contempt and curiosity. Design-snob: judges the page's own typography as evidence of the seller's taste.

panel.name: Ahmed
panel.profile: Uber driver, Detroit, ex-warehouse. Listens to Hormozi/Iman clips between rides. Buys 2-3 cheap biz courses a year, $37 is a day's tips. Needs to believe HE can do it without design skills — scans for 'no experience needed' reassurance and proof it's not US-only.

panel.name: Brigitte
panel.profile: Office admin, Lyon France. Bought Youzign for church flyers. English is fine but US-hype register puts her off; trusts European sellers more (Belgium is a plus she'll actually notice). Cautious: watches a thing twice before buying once.

panel.name: Kevin
panel.profile: Serial MMO buyer, Sydney. $15k lifetime spend on courses, owns every AI tool launched since 2023. Joins every launch JV list. Buys fast, refunds fast if the members area looks thin. The 'keep the bonuses' guarantee genuinely moves him — he's exploited it before.

panel.name: Grace
panel.profile: Nurse, Manila, side-hustles as social media VA for two US clients. Active Upworker with a 100% JSS on small contracts. Money tight, ambition high, evenings free. The realistic one: she'll do the 100 proposals if the path is concrete. Worries US clients won't take PH freelancers seriously for 'creative' work.

panel.name: Bob
panel.profile: Print-shop owner, Iowa, business slowly dying. Bought Youzign to make customer proofs. Sees AI as the thing killing him; this letter would have to flip that resentment into 'I could sell AI photos to MY existing customers'. Stubborn but has real client relationships most buyers lack.

panel.name: Sandra
panel.profile: Corporate HR, Atlanta, golden handcuffs. Dreams of escape, buys 'freedom' products, implements nothing because evenings vanish. Her test: does the page make the work feel schedulable into 5 hours a week, or does it smell like a second job?

panel.name: Dmitri
panel.profile: QA tester, Tbilisi (relocated). Technical, methodical, already uses Midjourney well. Doesn't need the craft taught — needs the CLIENT side: proposals, pricing, retainer mechanics. Will buy if the stack clearly sells the business system, not the prompting.

panel.name: Paula
panel.profile: Stay-at-home mom re-entering work, Birmingham UK. Confidence is the blocker: 'who am I to charge brands?'. The Instant Portfolio Prompt is secretly the headline product for her. Needs the letter to say a beginner with zero clients is normal, not an afterthought.

panel.name: Frank
panel.profile: Retired sales manager, Tampa. Old-school direct-response literate — owns Ogilvy and Caples paperbacks. Respects a well-built letter, despises fake countdown timers (he checks if they reset). Has disposable income; buys quality info products as entertainment as much as action.

panel.name: Nadia
panel.profile: Fiverr seller (logo gigs, $15), Cairo. Young, hungry, design-capable, drowning in race-to-the-bottom pricing. Upwork intimidates her. $37 is significant but she's spent more on Fiverr ads. Wants out of the $15 trap; the $90/hr math is the line she'll reread three times.

deep_cynics.name: Walt — the burned serial buyer
deep_cynics.profile: 59, Ohio, $11k spent on JVZoo/WarriorPlus offers over 12 years, a graveyard of logins. Can recite the Belcher formula beats from memory as they happen ('here comes the value stack'). Default position: 'this is the same $37 dream in a new wrapper.' The ONLY thing that's ever moved him: proof that can't be faked and a seller who admits the work involved.

deep_cynics.name: Donna — the broke skeptic
deep_cynics.profile: 44, single mom, Tennessee, $37 is groceries Thursday. Reads the whole page twice before any purchase. Every claim gets the 'yeah but YOU did that, not me' filter. Terrified of needing the refund and being made to feel cheap for asking. Looks for hidden costs: tools, subscriptions, Upwork fees, time.

deep_cynics.name: Greg — the busy day-jobber
deep_cynics.profile: 39, project manager, Denver, 50-hour weeks, two kids under 6. Has maybe 4 honest hours a week, 10pm-midnight. Auto-translates every promise into hours: 'make the sample in minutes' — sure, but how long to get GOOD enough that the sample wins? Abandons pages that dodge the learning-curve question.

deep_cynics.name: Eleanor — the tech-averse boomer
deep_cynics.profile: 64, retired teacher, Oregon. Bought Youzign in 2015, still uses it. No Upwork account, no AI tools, types with two fingers. Every unexplained term (retainer? milestone? prompt?) is a small door closing. Will not buy anything that assumes she already knows the platforms — but is surprisingly determined when steps are numbered.

deep_cynics.name: Vince — the marketer & design snob
deep_cynics.profile: 37, runs a small funnel agency, Austin. Has built 40 tripwire pages himself. Judges everything: headline mechanics, proof placement, congruence between the claim and the craft of the page itself ('you sell AI design but this page is...?'). Spots invented urgency, stock personas, and inflated value anchors instantly. Buys competitor products often, purely as recon — but only from operators who clearly know what they're doing.

vote_prompt_contract: Each persona outputs: VERDICT (BUY / MAYBE / NO), PRIMARY_OBJECTION (one sentence), LINE_THAT_LOST_ME or LINE_THAT_WON_ME (quote from the letter), FLIP_CONDITION (the single change most likely to flip the verdict).
